evals.name: full-repo-scan-default-scope
evals.prompt: Check docs for quality issues across the whole repo
evals.expected_output: Announces doc-quality-guard usage, scans docs/ and README.md files, dispatches parallel agents, prints a severity-classified report table, generates a fix spec, and asks for approval before invoking orchestrate
expectations: Announces 'Using doc-quality-guard to audit documentation...' at the start
Resolves default scope to docs/ and README.md files when no path argument is given
Uses Glob to collect all .md files in the resolved scope
Dispatches up to 4 parallel agents using the Agent tool, each covering a distinct doc scope
Each agent prompt includes the accuracy checklist (wrong API signatures, removed features, stale examples, wrong parameter tables, dead links, outdated install instructions)
Each agent prompt includes the 8 AI slop patterns (AI vocabulary, promotional language, filler phrases, chatbot tone, excessive hedging, em dash overuse, generic conclusions, copula avoidance)
Prints a report with severity categories: critical, high, medium, low
Generates a fix spec file at dev-docs/superpowers/specs/ with the date-stamped filename pattern
anti_expectations: Does not fix documentation files directly — only generates a spec and delegates to /orchestrate
Does not skip the approval gate — must use AskUserQuestion before invoking /orchestrate
Does not check external links (only internal relative links)

evals.name: scoped-scan-specific-directory
evals.prompt: Audit docs quality for docs/providers/ only
evals.expected_output: Scopes scan to docs/providers/, dispatches only the relevant agent (Agent A: Providers), cross-references against provider source code, prints report, generates spec if issues found
expectations: Scopes scan to docs/providers/ based on the user-provided path argument
Only dispatches agents whose scope overlaps with docs/providers/ — does not dispatch all 4 agents
Agent prompt cross-references provider docs against packages/tarash-gateway/src/tarash/tarash_gateway/providers/
Agent prompt includes tone reference instructions pointing to docs/getting-started/quickstart.md and docs/providers/openai.md
Agent output follows the structured format with file, issues, type, severity, line, description, and suggested_fix fields
Deduplicates findings: same file + same line keeps the highest severity entry
Report table includes columns: File, Line, Issue, Suggested Fix
anti_expectations: Does not scan docs outside the specified scope (e.g., docs/guides/, docs/api-reference/, README.md)
Does not flag style issues like formatting or naming conventions as critical or high severity
Does not proceed to /orchestrate without user approval via AskUserQuestion

evals.name: clean-docs-early-exit
evals.prompt: Check docs in docs/getting-started/ for any quality issues
evals.expected_output: Scans docs/getting-started/, dispatches the relevant agent, and if zero issues are found prints 'Docs are clean' message and stops without generating a spec or invoking orchestrate
expectations: Announces 'Using doc-quality-guard to audit documentation...' at the start
Resolves scope to docs/getting-started/ based on user argument
Dispatches the appropriate agent (Agent B: Guides + Getting Started) for the scoped path
If all agents find zero issues, prints 'Docs are clean — no accuracy or tone issues detected.'
If zero issues found, does NOT generate a fix spec file
If zero issues found, does NOT invoke /orchestrate
Reports error and stops if the scoped path does not exist or contains no .md files
anti_expectations: Does not generate a fix spec when zero issues are found
Does not invoke /orchestrate or AskUserQuestion when zero issues are found
Does not track quality over time — treats the run as a fresh scan
